question: [File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: sendBNB / receive] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (receiver contract) under PRECONDITIONS (attacker is the recipient of a sendBNB call triggered by a legitimate liquidation or redemption) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker's fallback re-enters ActivePool.sendBNB via a path that calls TroveManager.liquidate or BorrowerOperations.closeTrove before the first sendBNB returns), violating INVARIANT (ActivePool.BNB tracker must equal sum of all legitimate outstanding BNB obligations), causing scoped impact: attacker extracts more BNB from ActivePool than their entitlement? Local proof idea: write a Foundry reentrancy test where attacker's receive() calls back into a path that triggers another sendBNB, assert ActivePool.getBNB() < 0 or attacker balance > expected.

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: decreaseUSDSDebt / StabilityPool.offset] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (StabilityPool depositor who triggers offset) under PRECONDITIONS (StabilityPool.offset is callable only by TroveManager, but ActivePool.decreaseUSDSDebt is callable by StabilityPool directly) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker deploys a contract that impersonates StabilityPool address and calls activePool.decreaseUSDSDebt directly without a corresponding usdsToken.burn), violating INVARIANT (every call to decreaseUSDSDebt must be paired with an equal USDS burn), causing scoped impact: ActivePool USDSDebt is deflated without burning USDS, enabling future over-withdrawal of BNB? Local proof idea: unit test calling activePool.decreaseUSDSDebt from a mock StabilityPool without burning USDS, assert USDSDebt < actual outstanding debt.

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: receive / BorrowerOperations._activePoolAddColl] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (attacker force-sends BNB to ActivePool via selfdestruct, bypassing the receive() guard) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker selfdestructs a contract with BNB into ActivePool, then opens a trove and closes it, receiving only their own collateral), violating INVARIANT (ActivePool.address(this).balance must equal ActivePool.BNB tracker plus any force-sent dust), causing scoped impact: the force-sent BNB is permanently locked in ActivePool and cannot be extracted, but does the discrepancy allow any accounting path to over-withdraw? Local proof idea: fuzz test with selfdestruct injection, assert no user can withdraw more than their tracked BNB entitlement.

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: increaseUSDSDebt / BorrowerOperations._withdrawUSDS] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (borrower) under PRECONDITIONS (BorrowerOperations calls activePool.increaseUSDSDebt(netDebtIncrease) where netDebtIncrease includes the borrowing fee, but usdsToken.mint is called with only _USDSAmount excluding the fee) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker opens trove, observes that ActivePool.USDSDebt increases by netDebt+fee but attacker only receives _USDSAmount USDS, then redeems the full netDebt+fee worth of BNB), violating INVARIANT (USDS minted to borrower must equal USDS that can be used to redeem the corresponding BNB), causing scoped impact: attacker cannot extract extra BNB via this path, but verify the fee USDS minted to sableStaking is correctly accounted in USDSDebt? Local proof idea: unit test asserting usdsToken.totalSupply() == activePool.getUSDSDebt() + defaultPool.getUSDSDebt() after openTrove.

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: sendBNB / TroveManager._sendGasCompensation] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (liquidator) under PRECONDITIONS (batch liquidation of N troves, totalCollGasCompensation is accumulated across all troves) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker calls batchLiquidateTroves with a crafted array containing duplicate trove addresses, causing double-counting of gas compensation), violating INVARIANT (totalCollGasCompensation must not exceed 0.5% of total liquidated collateral), causing scoped impact: attacker receives more BNB gas compensation than entitled, draining ActivePool? Local proof idea: unit test batchLiquidateTroves with duplicate addresses, assert liquidator BNB gain <= 0.5% of total collateral.

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: sendBNB / TroveManager.redeemCollateral] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (redeemer) under PRECONDITIONS (BOOTSTRAP_PERIOD is 0 days, redemption is immediately available) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker opens a trove, immediately redeems their own USDS against another trove, receiving BNB at oracle price minus fee, then closes their own trove), violating INVARIANT (redeemer must not receive more BNB value than the USDS they burned plus the redemption fee), causing scoped impact: if oracleRate is 0 and baseRate is near 0, attacker extracts near-zero-fee BNB? Local proof idea: fuzz test with oracleRate=0 and baseRate=0, assert BNBFee >= REDEMPTION_FEE_FLOOR * totalBNBDrawn.

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: sendBNB / TroveManager._redeemCollateralFromTrove] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (redeemer) under PRECONDITIONS (partial redemption hint NICR is stale due to a pending redistribution reward on the target trove) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker calls redeemCollateral with a hint that matches pre-reward NICR, _applyPendingRewards updates the trove's coll and debt, newNICR != partialRedemptionHintNICR, redemption is cancelled), violating INVARIANT (cancelled partial redemption must not leave the redeemer with more BNB than they paid USDS for), causing scoped impact: attacker loses nothing but can they exploit the cancellation to extract value from a different trove in the same call? Local proof idea: unit test with stale hint, assert totals.totalBNBDrawn corresponds exactly to totals.totalUSDSToRedeem at oracle price.

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: sendBNB / TroveManager._redistributeDebtAndColl] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (totalStakes is very small, e.g. only one trove with minimal stake) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker liquidates a large trove causing _redistributeDebtAndColl with large _coll and _debt, BNBRewardPerUnitStaked = BNBNumerator / totalStakes overflows or is disproportionately large), violating INVARIANT (sum of all pending BNB rewards must equal DefaultPool.getBNB()), causing scoped impact: attacker's trove accumulates more pending BNB reward than DefaultPool holds, enabling over-withdrawal? Local proof idea: invariant fuzz test asserting sum(getPendingBNBReward(trove) for all troves) <= defaultPool.getBNB().

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: sendBNB / TroveManager._computeNewStake] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (attacker is the only remaining trove after a liquidation sequence, totalCollateralSnapshot is set to a very small value) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker opens a new trove after the liquidation, _computeNewStake computes stake = coll * totalStakesSnapshot / totalCollateralSnapshot with inflated ratio, attacker's stake is disproportionately large), violating INVARIANT (a trove's stake must not exceed its actual collateral contribution to the system), causing scoped impact: attacker claims disproportionate redistribution rewards from future liquidations, extracting more BNB than entitled? Local proof idea: unit test with controlled totalCollateralSnapshot, assert attacker's pending BNB reward <= their proportional share of redistributed collateral.

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: sendBNB / StabilityPool._moveOffsetCollAndDebt] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (StabilityPool depositor) under PRECONDITIONS (StabilityPool.offset is called, activePool.sendBNB(address(this), _collToAdd) is called, StabilityPool.receive() updates BNB tracker) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker's deposit snapshot is taken at epoch/scale before the offset, attacker calls withdrawFromSP immediately after offset, getDepositorBNBGain returns the full BNB gain), violating INVARIANT (depositor's BNB gain must not exceed their proportional share of offset collateral), causing scoped impact: rounding in _computeRewardsPerUnitStaked causes depositor to claim slightly more BNB than their share? Local proof idea: fuzz test with many small offsets, assert sum of all depositor BNB gains <= total BNB received by StabilityPool.

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: sendBNB / StabilityPool.withdrawFromSP] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (StabilityPool depositor) under PRECONDITIONS (_amount == 0, there exist undercollateralized troves in the system) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker calls withdrawFromSP(0, priceFeedData), _requireNoUnderCollateralizedTroves is skipped because _amount == 0, but _sendBNBGainToDepositor still executes and sends accumulated BNB gains to attacker), violating INVARIANT (BNB gains must not be extractable while undercollateralized troves exist that should be liquidated first), causing scoped impact: attacker extracts BNB gains from StabilityPool while bypassing the undercollateralized trove check, potentially destabilizing the liquidation buffer? Local proof idea: unit test calling withdrawFromSP(0,...) with an undercollateralized trove present, assert BNB is transferred to attacker despite the check being skipped.

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: sendBNB / StabilityPool.withdrawBNBGainToTrove] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (StabilityPool depositor with an active trove) under PRECONDITIONS (depositor has accumulated BNB gain, calls withdrawBNBGainToTrove) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (BNB is subtracted from SP.BNB tracker and sent to BorrowerOperations.moveBNBGainToTrove, which calls _adjustTrove for the borrower, which calls activePool.receive() increasing ActivePool.BNB, but the trove's collateral is also increased), violating INVARIANT (total system collateral = ActivePool.BNB + DefaultPool.BNB must remain consistent after the operation), causing scoped impact: double-counting of BNB in system collateral if the BNB gain was already counted in ActivePool? Local proof idea: assert getEntireSystemColl() is unchanged after withdrawBNBGainToTrove since BNB moves from SP to ActivePool.

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: sendBNB / CollSurplusPool.claimColl] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (attacker has a surplus balance in CollSurplusPool from a recovery-mode liquidation) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker calls BorrowerOperations.claimCollateral which calls collSurplusPool.claimColl(msg.sender), claimColl sets balances[_account] = 0 and sends BNB, but CollSurplusPool.BNB tracker is decremented by claimableColl), violating INVARIANT (CollSurplusPool.BNB tracker must equal sum of all balances[account]), causing scoped impact: if accountSurplus is called multiple times for the same borrower before claimColl, does the BNB tracker correctly reflect the cumulative surplus? Local proof idea: unit test with multiple accountSurplus calls for same address, assert CollSurplusPool.getBNB() == sum of all balances.

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: sendBNB / TroveManager._liquidateRecoveryMode getCappedOffsetVals] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (liquidator) under PRECONDITIONS (system is in recovery mode, target trove has ICR >= MCR and ICR < TCR, entireTroveDebt <= USDSInStabPool) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (TroveHelper.getCappedOffsetVals computes cappedCollPortion = debt * MCR / price, collSurplus = entireTroveColl - cappedCollPortion, attacker claims collSurplus via claimCollateral), violating INVARIANT (collSurplus must equal entireTroveColl - debt * MCR / price, and the sum collToSendToSP + collGasCompensation + collSurplus must equal entireTroveColl), causing scoped impact: rounding in cappedCollPortion calculation causes collSurplus to be slightly over-allocated, allowing borrower to claim more than their surplus? Local proof idea: fuzz test with random debt/coll/price values, assert collToSendToSP + collGasCompensation + collSurplus == entireTroveColl.

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: sendBNB / TroveManager.redeemCollateral _updateBaseRateFromRedemption] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (redeemer) under PRECONDITIONS (baseRate is near 0, MINUTE_DECAY_FACTOR causes rapid decay) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker splits a large redemption into many small redemptions across multiple blocks, each time baseRate decays before the next redemption, keeping the effective fee near the floor), violating INVARIANT (cumulative redemption fee must be >= REDEMPTION_FEE_FLOOR * totalBNBDrawn across all redemptions), causing scoped impact: attacker redeems large amounts of USDS for BNB at near-zero fee, extracting value from the protocol? Local proof idea: fuzz test with N small redemptions across time, assert sum of fees >= REDEMPTION_FEE_FLOOR * sum of BNB drawn.

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: sendBNB / BorrowerOperations.closeTrove] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (borrower) under PRECONDITIONS (borrower has pending redistribution rewards, calls closeTrove) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (applyPendingRewards moves BNB from DefaultPool to ActivePool and increases trove coll, then activePool.sendBNB(msg.sender, coll) sends the full coll including redistributed BNB), violating INVARIANT (DefaultPool.getBNB() must decrease by exactly pendingBNBReward when applyPendingRewards is called), causing scoped impact: if DefaultPool.BNB tracker is less than pendingBNBReward due to prior rounding errors, the sendBNBToActivePool call underflows? Local proof idea: invariant test asserting defaultPool.getBNB() >= sum of all getPendingBNBReward() across active troves.

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: sendBNB / BorrowerOperations._moveTokensAndBNBfromAdjustment] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (msg.sender != _borrower is only allowed for StabilityPool calling moveBNBGainToTrove, but the MoveTokensAndBNBFromAdjustmentParam sets borrower: msg.sender not _borrower) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (StabilityPool calls moveBNBGainToTrove for borrower B, _adjustTrove is called with _borrower=B but msg.sender=StabilityPool, if isDebtIncrease were true USDS would be minted to StabilityPool not B), violating INVARIANT (USDS minted must go to the trove owner, not the caller), causing scoped impact: in the current code isDebtIncrease is always false for moveBNBGainToTrove so no USDS is minted, but verify no path allows USDS to be minted to StabilityPool? Local proof idea: unit test asserting that moveBNBGainToTrove never mints USDS to stabilityPoolAddress.

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: sendBNB / TroveManager._getOffsetAndRedistributionVals] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (StabilityPool depositor) under PRECONDITIONS (USDSInStabPool is slightly less than trove debt, causing partial offset) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (collToSendToSP = coll * debtToOffset / debt rounds down, collToRedistribute = coll - collToSendToSP rounds up, the extra dust in collToRedistribute is redistributed to all stakers), violating INVARIANT (collToSendToSP + collToRedistribute must equal collToLiquidate exactly), causing scoped impact: rounding dust accumulates in redistribution, allowing stakers to claim fractionally more BNB than the liquidated collateral? Local proof idea: fuzz test asserting collToSendToSP + collToRedistribute == collToLiquidate for all input combinations.

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: sendBNB / StabilityPool._getBNBGainFromSnapshots] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (depositor who spans a scale change) under PRECONDITIONS (deposit was made at scale N, current scale is N+1, secondPortion = epochToScaleToSum[epoch][scale+1] / SCALE_FACTOR) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker makes a deposit just before a scale change, a liquidation occurs causing scale increment, attacker calls withdrawFromSP and receives BNBGain = initialDeposit * (firstPortion + secondPortion) / P_Snapshot / DECIMAL_PRECISION), violating INVARIANT (sum of all depositor BNB gains must not exceed total BNB received by StabilityPool from liquidations), causing scoped impact: the secondPortion calculation adds S[scale+1]/SCALE_FACTOR which could double-count BNB gains if S[scale+1] was already partially included in firstPortion? Local proof idea: unit test with controlled scale change, assert sum of all BNB gains == total BNB in StabilityPool.

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: sendBNB / StabilityPool._computeRewardsPerUnitStaked] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (depositor) under PRECONDITIONS (lastBNBError_Offset accumulates over many small liquidations) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (many small liquidations each add a small BNBNumerator error to lastBNBError_Offset, after N liquidations the accumulated error causes BNBGainPerUnitStaked to be inflated by up to 1 wei per liquidation), violating INVARIANT (sum of all BNB gains distributed to depositors must not exceed total BNB sent to StabilityPool), causing scoped impact: accumulated rounding errors allow depositors to collectively claim more BNB than the pool received? Local proof idea: fuzz test with 1000 small liquidations, assert sum of getDepositorBNBGain() for all depositors <= StabilityPool.getBNB().

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: sendBNB / StabilityPool.getCompoundedUSDSDeposit scaleDiff>=2] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (depositor) under PRECONDITIONS (depositor's deposit spans 2+ scale changes, getCompoundedUSDSDeposit returns 0) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker calls withdrawFromSP, compoundedUSDSDeposit=0, USDStoWithdraw=0, but depositorBNBGain is still calculated using _getBNBGainFromSnapshots which uses initialDeposit not compoundedDeposit), violating INVARIANT (a depositor with compounded deposit of 0 should have proportionally zero BNB gain), causing scoped impact: attacker extracts BNB gains even though their deposit is treated as 0, receiving BNB without a corresponding USDS deposit? Local proof idea: unit test with 2 scale changes, assert getDepositorBNBGain() == 0 when getCompoundedUSDSDeposit() == 0.

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: sendBNB / StabilityPool._payOutSABLEGains] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (front-end operator) under PRECONDITIONS (front-end has accumulated SABLE gains, multiple depositors tagged to the same front-end call provideToSP or withdrawFromSP in the same block) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (each depositor call triggers _payOutSABLEGains which calls getFrontEndSABLEGain using the current frontEndStakes before _updateFrontEndStakeAndSnapshots, causing the front-end gain to be paid out multiple times before the snapshot is updated), violating INVARIANT (front-end SABLE gains must be paid out exactly once per issuance period), causing scoped impact: front-end operator receives more SABLE than their proportional share? Local proof idea: unit test with 3 depositors calling withdrawFromSP in same block, assert total SABLE paid to front-end == getFrontEndSABLEGain() at start of block.

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: sendBNB / TroveManager.redeemCollateral] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (redeemer) under PRECONDITIONS (redeemer holds USDS equal to total system debt, BOOTSTRAP_PERIOD=0) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker redeems all USDS, all troves are closed, ActivePool.BNB is drained to redeemer minus fees, but the last trove cannot be closed per requireMoreThanOneTroveInSystem), violating INVARIANT (at least one trove must remain in the system after redemption), causing scoped impact: if the last-trove guard in _closeTrove is bypassed during redemption, the system becomes insolvent? Local proof idea: unit test attempting to redeem against the last trove, assert _closeTrove reverts with requireMoreThanOneTroveInSystem.

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: sendBNB / BorrowerOperations.openTrove] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (borrower) under PRECONDITIONS (system is in recovery mode, attacker opens a trove with ICR exactly at CCR) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker opens trove with ICR=CCR, no borrowing fee is charged in recovery mode, attacker receives _USDSAmount USDS without paying the fee, then immediately withdraws collateral to bring ICR back to CCR), violating INVARIANT (in recovery mode, new troves must not worsen the system TCR), causing scoped impact: attacker mints USDS at zero fee in recovery mode, extracting value from the protocol? Local proof idea: unit test in recovery mode, assert USDSFee == 0 and verify TCR does not decrease after openTrove.

[File: 'src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain'] [Function: sendBNB / BorrowerOperations.adjustTrove] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (borrower) under PRECONDITIONS (system transitions from recovery mode to normal mode mid-transaction due to price update) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker calls adjustTrove with collateral withdrawal, isRecoveryMode is checked at start of _adjustTrove, but by the time _requireValidAdjustmentInCurrentMode is called the mode may have changed), violating INVARIANT (collateral withdrawal must not be permitted in recovery mode), causing scoped impact: attacker withdraws collateral during recovery mode by exploiting the single mode-check at transaction start? Local proof idea: unit test where price drops between mode check and adjustment validation, assert collateral withdrawal reverts.
